data.title: ViiV reports positive real-world data for HIV prevention drug
data.description:
• ViiV Healthcare, which is co-owned by GSK (NYSE:GSK), Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and Shionogi, said real-world studies showed its drug Apretude was more than 99% effective as a pre-exposure prophylaxis, or PrEP, treatment for the prevention of HIV infection.
  • The company said the data underscored the high adherence rate and effectiveness of Apretude, a long-acting formulation of cabotegravir. The data will be presented at the IDWeek 2024 conference, which is being held Oct. 16-19 in Los Angeles, according to a statement.
  • ViiV is majority owned by GSK (GSK). Pfizer (PFE) and Shionogi are also shareholders.

data.title: Least shorted S&P 500 stocks in September
data.description:
Amentum Holdings (AMTM), Walmart (WMT) and Philip Morris International (PM) took the top spots as the least shorted S&P 500 (SP500) stocks at September end.

Engineering solutions firm Amentum (AMTM), a new inclusion to the index, had a short interest of 376.11K shares as of Sep 30, representing 0.24% of its float. The stock is the newest addition to the S&P 500 after a spinoff from engineering and construction company Jacobs Solutions.

Philip Morris (PM) had a short interest of 8.33M shares, representing 0.54% of its float. The tobacco company saw an 18% decrease in short share volume during the month compared to Aug-end.

L3Harris Technologies (LHX) took the third spot, with a short interest of 1.12M shares as of Sep 30, representing 0.59% of the float. The defense contractor saw an 8.7% increase in short share volume during the month.

These were the top 20 least shorted S&P 500 stocks as a percentage of float, as of September:

  • Amentum Holdings (AMTM) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.24%
  • Philip Morris International (PM) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.54%
  • L3Harris Technologies Inc (LHX) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.59%
  • Mastercard (MA) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.60%
  • Allstate Corp (ALL) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.66%
  • Eli Lilly & Co (LLY) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.64%
  • Abbott Laboratories (ABT) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.68%
  • Union Pacific (UNP) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.70%
  • Walmart (WMT) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.71%
  • Boston Scientific (BSX) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.74%
  • American Tower (AMT) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.77%
  • Coca-Cola Co (KO) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.79%
  • Ecolab (ECL) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.80%
  • Arista Networks (ANET) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.80%
  • Microsoft (MSFT) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.80%
  • Progressive Corp (PGR) - Short interest as a % of float: 0.81%

data.title: BlackBerry shows plan to return to profitability next year at investor day
data.description:
BlackBerry (NYSE:BB) showed off its plan to boost growth and profitability at its investor day on Wednesday, including establishing "virtually autonomous divisions."

The plan, which was unveiled at the New York Stock Exchange, includes added profitability details for the Company's IoT and cybersecurity division. Looking to 2025, BlackBerry said it expects overall revenue to be between $591M and $616M, with the midpoint roughly in line with the $604M estimate.

The company's Internet of Things division should generate sales ranging between $225M and $235M next year, with the $230M midpoint slightly below the $231M estimate. BlackBerry also said that its cybersecurity business should break-even next year, while licensing revenue should account for $16M in revenue.

Adjusted EBITDA for next year should be $0 to $10M, below the $12.5M estimate. Adjusted gross margin for the company should be around 71%.

Looking to the future

BlackBerry also provided revenue and profitability forecasts for each division and for the company as a whole for fiscal 2026 and 2027. Revenue for 2026 should be between roughly $620M and $650M, while sales for 2027 should be roughly $655M to $685M. Adjusted EBITDA for 2026 should be roughly $50M to $65M

The company also outlined its capital allocation priorities and strategic direction, including streamlining its investment in Cylance, for which it is exploring options.

"Today's Investor Day event showcased the tremendous progress we've made as a company since last December, both in terms of how we've positioned our IoT and Cybersecurity divisions to focus on their market opportunities, as well as the positive transformation in our financial performance," BlackBerry Chief Executive John J. Giamatteo said. "As a new management team, we've taken a fresh look at our capital allocation priorities, increasing focus on BlackBerry's growth engines, particularly QNX."

data.title: Freshworks initiated at Equal Weight as it transitions to ITSM: Wells Fargo
data.description:
Freshworks (NASDAQ:FRSH) was initiated with an Equal Weight rating by Wells Fargo as the firm transitions from a small and medium-sized business customer service and management software provider to an IT service management system as well.

Wells Fargo also set a price target of $11 on Freshworks. Freshworks' share price has plunged 75% since it became a publicly traded company in October 2021. It has dropped 51% year to date. It was down another 1% by noon trading on Wednesday.

"We view Freshworks as targeting a sizeable $75B TAM across its 3 key businesses," said Wells Fargo analysts, led by Michael Berg, in a Wednesday note. "With a shift of focus to ITSM (from Customer Support & CRM), we believe FRSH is targeting the market that is seeing the fastest growth (high-teens CAGR) with our diligence suggesting the mid-market/low enterprise cohort that is highly cost conscious is ripe for FRSH to disrupt and fit in longer term."

Freshworks is a cloud-based software company that provides artificial intelligence-powered platforms for customer service, IT, sales and marketing.

The business runs efficiently and with low operating costs, and its diverse product line has allowed it to maintain top-line growth of about 20% for the past five quarters. However, Wells Fargo doesn't see a clear catalyst in the near term. Freshworks also operates in a highly competitive market.

"FRSH has a significant portion of its business in exposed end markets (primarily SMBs), which are currently experiencing elevated headwinds," Berg noted. "In our view, these are likely to persist in 2024 and beyond, creating a headwind to top-line growth."

Last month, Seeking Alpha contributor GS Investing recommended a Buy rating for Freshworks due to its consistent revenue growth coupled with a low valuation.

data.title: Orange juice prices soar as industry swamped by diseased trees, weather, development
data.description:
As consumers struggle with higher prices for groceries, one staple of the American breakfast is about to get a lot more expensive. Plagued by diseased groves, uprooted trees to make room for housing developments, and severe weather that has decimated crops, the price of orange juice has skyrocketed in the past 3 years by 327% and is expected to climb even higher in the wake of Hurricanes Helene and Milton which swept through Florida less than three weeks apart.

According to industry data, Hurricane Milton destroyed over 3 million boxes of oranges in Florida, setting the state up for the smallest orange harvest in close to 100 years. Coupled with a protracted drought in Brazil, the world’s largest orange exporter, the price for frozen concentrated orange juice trading on the Intercontinental Commodity Exchange (“ICE”) has soared 80% to a record high of $5.25 per pound.

Before Milton and Helene visited the state, Florida was already grappling with a bacterial disease called citrus greening that swept through orange groves, producing fruit that is bitter, misshaped, and poorly colored, and eventually killing the tree. Farmers have resorted to building expensive greenhouses over an entire orchard to protect the crop from the disease spread by the Asian citrus psyllid, an insect which transmits the disease while they feed. Since 2005, citrus greening has reduced citrus production by 75% and doubled production costs.

Faced with diseased trees and the risk of another hurricane wiping out their crop, many of Florida’s orange farmers have thrown in the towel and sold off valuable land to real estate developers who were rapidly putting up housing developments where orchards once stood. Land devoted to citrus production in Florida has shrunk by 17% while the price of an undeveloped acre of land in Polk County (the largest orange producing county in the state) has appreciated by more than 60% since 2014.

Most orange juice sold in grocery stores -- including Tropicana, the most popular brand and 39% owned by PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) -- is made from a process called “not-from-concentrate” which involves squeezing fresh oranges and then filtering and pasteurizing the juice before bottling it. Coca-Cola’s (NYSE:KO) Simply Orange is also made from the same process, while Minute Maid (KO) is made from a cheaper process using concentrate.

To address the higher price for oranges, Tropicana has reduced the size of a container of juice by 10% and increased the price by more than 30% from a year ago. And while Coca-Cola (KO) does not separate out sales for Simply Orange or Minute Maid, the company earns roughly $13B annually from its juice drinks globally, with Simply Orange generating over $2B in annual sales for the company.

Since it’s unlikely producers and retailers will absorb the higher cost of oranges, a 46oz bottle of Tropicana could reach $4. At $5, however, a negative demand response could recalibrate demand with supply to the advantage of the consumer.

data.title: Enphase Energy cut at Susquehanna on market share concerns, high valuation
data.description:
Enphase Energy (NASDAQ:ENPH) +0.4% in Wednesday's trading despite receiving a downgrade at Susquehanna to Neutral from Positive with a $104 price target, cut from $147, citing the slower than expected recovery in U.S. residentials and market share concerns, coupled with the stock's premium valuation.

Susquehanna's Biju Perincheril says his recent conversations indicate project delays remain a headwind for utility-scale projects, with utility-focused names at risk of seeing further pushouts into 2025 as project cycle times expand, and election uncertainty also has weighed on some project sanctioning, particularly with smaller developers.

The analyst says his concern for Enphase (ENPH) is that the increasingly competitive landscape could make it difficult for the company to maintain its superior market share and margin leadership in the inverter market, which could weigh on the stock's premium valuation.

Enphase's (ENPH) inverter market share nationally on an installed basis has remained at ~50% for the last several quarters according to third-party data, but Perincheril says leading indicators such as EnergySage's quote data shows Enphase's market share being impacted by Powerwall 3 penetration.

data.title: Ascendis Pharma ticks higher amid report it cancelled from upcoming conference
data.description:
Ascendis Pharma (ASND) edged higher by 1.6% on chatter that the pharma company cancelled from a conference next month.

Ascendis (ASND) is said to have cancelled from a Stifel healthcare conference in New York City scheduled for Nov. 18-19 due to a scheduling conflict, according to chatter circulating among traders on Wednesday.

Ascendis (ASND) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha email request for comment.

data.title: BlackRock spotlights investment opportunities in Japan, China, and U.K.
data.description:
BlackRock observes some compelling opportunities in global equities that are currently trading at relatively attractive valuations in areas such as Japan, China, and the United Kingdom.

BlackRock highlighted that it remains overweight Japanese stocks as the world’s largest asset manager stated: “The macro outlook [for Japan] is brightening and corporate reforms are driving improved corporate earnings and shareholder returns.”

As for the United Kingdom, the financial institution also stays overweight but does bring up some concerns. “We are overweight UK stocks, but have less conviction as the economy stagnates and heading into the new government’s budget announcement.”

China is the other avenue BlackRock is focused on, as signs of major fiscal policy stimulus are on the way. While being “modestly overweight, in China, BlackRock said, “that doesn’t change the long-term, structural challenges we are concerned about.”

For investors looking to expand from U.S. exposures, they can look towards specific nation-focused exchange traded funds. While BlackRock did not specifically mention names, Seeking Alpha constructed a list of potential investment options that would fall within exposure towards Japan, the United Kingdom, and China. See below:

Japan ETFs: (EWJ), (BBJP), (DXJ), (FLJP), (FXY), (DBJP), (HEWJ), (EWJV), (DFJ), (JPXN), (SCJ), (DXJS), (FLJH), (YCL), and (YCS).

U.K. ETFs: (EWU), (FLGB), (FKU), (FXB), and (EWUS).

China ETFs: (YANG), (KLIP), (CNYA), (ASHR), (KBA), (PGJ), (CQQQ), (CXSE), (GXC), (CHIQ), (CWEB), (YINN), (MCHI), (KWEB), and (FXI).

data.title: Sanofi unions to strike in France over proposed unit sale: report
data.description:
• Sanofi’s (NASDAQ:SNY) two largest unions in France reportedly plan to go on strike Wednesday over the proposed sale of a controlling stake in the company’s consumer healthcare unit Opella to private equity firm Clayton Dubilier & Rice for around $16B.
  • According to Reuters, the strike was called by the labor unions CGT and CFDT and will cover all of France. At issue are concerns that the deal could result in the loss of jobs and domestic production of certain medications like the pain reliever Doliprane.
  • France’s economic minister, Antoine Armand, said on Monday that his government will need certain guarantees that Doliprane production will remain in France in order for the sale to proceed, Reuters added.
  • Last week, Sanofi confirmed that it was in negotiations to sell a 50% controlling stake in Opella to Clayton Dubilier & Rice. The consumer healthcare unit, which markets such brands as Allegra and Ducolax, employs over 11,000 people and operates in 100 countries.
  • Bloomberg reported last week that Sanofi was close to selling a majority stake in Opella for around EUR 15B, or $16.4B.

data.title: Netflix subscriber addition, ad-tier growth in focus during Q3 results
data.description:
Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) is set to report third quarter results on Thursday, and investors will keep an eye on subscribers' trend and the performance of the streaming giant’s cheaper advertising tier.

Wall Street expects the California-based company to post EPS of $5.13, while revenue is expected to rise 14.3% to $9.76 billion during the quarter.

Netflix likely added 5.5 million net subscribers globally during the third quarter, versus consensus of 4.5 million, according to Wedbush. The streaming pioneer added 8.8 million subscribers a year earlier, which included the first full quarter impact of paid sharing.

The company plans to stop reporting subscribers number starting in the first quarter of 2025.

Despite the decelerating subscribers’ numbers, analysts are still bullish on the company’s future growth, with many anticipating planned price hikes.

Citi said it sees scope for the firm to raise U.S. prices by 12% in 2025, while Oppenheimer anticipates a hike in Premium pricing for other regions after the company’s announced price hikes in the U.S., UK, and France in October.

Earlier in July, Netflix surpassed revenue growth and user growth expectations in its second-quarter earnings.

“We think Netflix is positioned to accelerate ad tier revenue contribution into year-end and 2025 as it improves its advertising solutions and targeting, utilizes new partnerships, and adds more live events,” said Wedbush analyst Alicia Reese.

Reese also thinks Netflix will ramp up its content slate over the holiday, attracting more viewers while also managing content costs.

The company’s content slate for the quarter included a brand-new season of Emily in Paris, The Perfect Couple and The Accident, among others, while the fourth quarter will see the release of the second season of the popular South Korean drama series Squid Game.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen 30 upward revisions, compared to one downward revision. Revenue estimates have been revised upward 13 times versus 19 downward moves.

Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street are bullish and rated the stock a Buy, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings consider it a Hold.

The stock gained nearly 44% so far this year, outperforming the 22% rise in the S&P500 Index.
